title: PROP-023 type-level assertion is tautological — does not catch hydrateFeed gaining a Timestamp parameter
description: PROP-023 (verification-architecture.md:147) explicitly says: 'verify hydrateFeed call signature has no Timestamp parameter via TypeScript type assertion'. PROP-023 is the only Tier-1 required:true proof obligation added in this revision and underwrites the post-FIND-003 purity boundary claim. The implementation in step3-hydrate-feed.test.ts:42-51 encodes the assertion as: type _NoTimestampParam = Extract<_HydrateFeedSig[0], { epochMillis: number }>; const _noTimestamp: _NoTimestampParam = undefined as never;. This is tautological because the value `undefined as never` is assignable to ANY type — including Timestamp-shaped types. If a future change rewrites hydrateFeed as `(scannedVault: ScannedVault, now: Timestamp)`, _NoTimestampParam would become `Timestamp` (not `never`), and the line `const _noTimestamp: Timestamp = undefined as never` would still compile (never is the bottom type). Therefore the compile-time guard does not exist in any operational sense. The runtime arity check `hydrateFeed.length === 1` partially compensates but only catches first-position changes; it does not enforce parameter-type purity, and PROP-023 explicitly demands the type-level guarantee for the Tier-1 required:true claim.
remediation: Replace the always-passing assertion with a compile-time guard such as: `type _ParamCount = _HydrateFeedSig['length']; const _len: _ParamCount = 1 as const;` or `const _check: ScannedVault = (null as unknown as _HydrateFeedSig[0]);` so that adding a second Timestamp parameter would change the inferred parameter tuple and break the assignment. Equivalently: `type _AssertNoSecondParam = _HydrateFeedSig extends [unknown] ? true : false; const _nope: _AssertNoSecondParam = true;` would fail to compile if the function arity grew.
